expectations.text: Output recommends a named tier (Minimal, Standard, or Strict)
expectations.evidence: Output has 'Recommended Tier: Strict' with clear rationale about operational blast radius

expectations.text: Tier is Standard or Strict — 4-person team with production incidents warrants escalated governance
expectations.evidence: Recommended Strict, with reasoning that the 6am ETL incidents classify the risk as High

expectations.text: Branch protection is highlighted as the most urgent or highest-priority fix (not buried at the end)
expectations.evidence: Branch protection is the 5th item (after pre-commit framework, black, ruff, hygiene hooks). However, the Why text says 'this alone would have stopped the incidents you've been having' which gives it clear urgency even in 5th position.

expectations.text: Output includes CI GitHub Actions workflow snippet
Output includes pre-commit hook config with black and ruff
Output explains WHY items matter in terms of preventing the type of incident described
expectations.evidence: Coverage enforcement Why: 'exactly the kind of gap that causes 6am incidents'. Branch protection Why: 'this alone would have stopped the incidents you've been having'

expectations.text: Output includes effort estimates for items

notes: Only failure: branch protection isn't first. The skill prioritizes pre-commit before branch protection per the 'order of operations' principle, but for an existing team already in production, immediate branch protection should arguably be first.
